entries.definition:
كتاب الهمزة

أيم

(أيي) الهمزة والياء والياء أصلٌ واحد، وهو النَّظَر . يقال تأيَّا يتأيَّا تَأَيِّياً، أي تمكَّث. قال:

وتأَيَّ إنّك غيرُ صاغرْ

قِفْ بالدِّيار وقوفَ زائرْ

قال لبيد:

وعلى الأرض غَيَايَاتُ الطَّفَلْ

وتأَيَّيْتُ عليه قَافِلاً

أي انصرفتُ على تُؤَدة. ابن الأعرابيّ: تأيّيْت [الأمرَ ]: انتظرت إمكانَه. قال عديّ:

they will ever be raised up.
أَيْن ʾayna interrogative pronoun of (اسم استفهام للمكان) ‘where?’, occurring seven times in the Qurʾan and regularly used rhetorically to mean ‘nowhere!’, ‘is there ever a place?!’, ‘wherever?’ (75:10) يَقُولُ ٱلْإِنسَٰنُ يَوْمَئِذٍ أَيْنَ ٱلْمَفَرُّ on that Day man will say, ‘Wherever is the escape?!’

أَيْنَمَا ʾaynamā conditional (شرطية) (considered by many grammarians to be a compound of conditional أَيْن + mā of ambiguity (ما المبهمة)), denoting emphasis ‘wherever!’ (4:78) أَيْنَمَا تَكُونُوا۟ يُدْرِككُّمُ ٱلْمَوْتُ wherever you may be, death will overtake you.
entries.definition_english:
they will ever be raised up.
أَيْن ʾayna interrogative pronoun of (اسم استفهام للمكان) ‘where?’, occurring seven times in the Qurʾan and regularly used rhetorically to mean ‘nowhere!’, ‘is there ever a place?!’, ‘wherever?’ (75:10) يَقُولُ ٱلْإِنسَٰنُ يَوْمَئِذٍ أَيْنَ ٱلْمَفَرُّ on that Day man will say, ‘Wherever is the escape?!’

أَيْنَمَا ʾaynamā conditional (شرطية) (considered by many grammarians to be a compound of conditional أَيْن + mā of ambiguity (ما المبهمة)), denoting emphasis ‘wherever!’ (4:78) أَيْنَمَا تَكُونُوا۟ يُدْرِككُّمُ ٱلْمَوْتُ wherever you may be, death will overtake you.

entries.definition:
ʾ–y–y (1) marker, sign, token, indicator, to mark; to pause, to tarry; to ponder, to assure oneself; a person’s body; glory; light of the sun; beauty of flowers. Of this root, three forms occur 380 times in the Qurʾan: آيَة ʾāyatun 86 times; آيَتَيْن ʾāyatayn once and آيَات ʾāyāt 295 times.
آيَة ʾāyatun [n.; dual آيَتَيْن ʾāyatayn; pl. آيَات ʾāyāt] 1 sign, indicator, indication (12:105) وَكَأَيِّن مِّنْ ءَايَةٍ فِى ٱلسَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ يَمُرُّونَ عَلَيْهَا وَهُمْ عَنْهَا مُعْرِضُونَ and how many a sign is there in the heavens and the Earth which they pass by, turning away from it! 2 evidence, proof (12:35) ثُمَّ بَدَا لَهُم مِّنۢ بَعْدِ مَا رَأَوُا۟ ٱلْءَايَٰتِ لَيَسْجُنُنَّهُۥ حَتَّىٰ حِينٍ but then it occurred to them, [even] after seeing the evidence [for his innocence], that they should imprison him for a while 3 miracle, portent (13:7) وَيَقُولُ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ لَوْلَآ أُنزِلَ عَلَيْهِ ءَايَةٌ مِّن رَّبِّهِۦ and those who disbelieve say, ‘If only some portent were sent down upon him from his Lord?’ 4 symbol, exemplar (23:50) وَجَعَلْنَا ٱبْنَ مَرْيَمَ وَأُمَّهُۥٓ ءَايَةً and We made the son of Mary and his mother an exemplar 5 message, revelation (20:134) رَبَّنَا لَوْلَآ أَرْسَلْتَ إِلَيْنَا رَسُولًا فَنَتَّبِعَ ءَايَٰتِكَ مِن قَبْلِ أَن نَّذِلَّ وَنَخْزَىٰ Lord, if only You had sent us a messenger, so that we might have followed Your revelations, before we suffered humiliation and disgrace! 6 teachings, instructions (24:1) سُورَةٌ أَنزَلْنَٰهَا وَفَرَضْنَٰهَا وَأَنزَلْنَا فِيهَآ ءَايَٰتٍ بَيِّنَٰتٍ this is a sura We have sent down and made obligatory–We have sent down clear instruction in it, so that you may take heed 7 verses, parts of the Qurʾan (16:101) وَإِذَا بَدَّلْنَآ ءَايَةً مَّكَانَ ءَايَةٍ وَٱللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ بِمَا يُنَزِّلُ قَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّمَآ أَنتَ مُفْتَرٍ when We substitute one verse for another–and God knows best what He reveals–they say, ‘You are just making it up’ 8

69

أَيّ

best what He reveals–they say, ‘You are just making it up’ 8 lesson (3:13) قَدْ كَانَ لَكُمْ ءَايَةٌ فِى فِئَتَيْنِ ٱلْتَقَتَا you have already seen a lesson in the two armies that met [in battle] 9 glory, wonder (17:1) سُبْحَٰنَ ٱلَّذِىٓ أَسْرَىٰ بِعَبْدِهِۦ لَيْلًا مِّنَ ٱلْمَسْجِدِ ٱلْحَرَامِ إِلَى ٱلْمَسْجِدِ ٱلْأَقْصَا ٱلَّذِى بَٰرَكْنَا حَوْلَهُۥ لِنُرِيَهُۥ مِنْ ءَايَٰتِنَآ glory be to Him who made His servant travel by night from the Sacred Mosque to the Furthest Mosque, whose surroundings We have blessed, to show him some of Our wonders 10 spell, in an interpretation of verse (7:132) وَقَالُوا۟ مَهْمَا تَأْتِنَا بِهِۦ مِنْ ءَايَةٍ لِّتَسْحَرَنَا بِهَا فَمَا نَحْنُ لَكَ بِمُؤْمِنِينَ and they said ‘Whatever spell [also interpreted as: sign] you produce in order to enchant us with, we will never believe with you.’

أَيّ ʾayy (2) occurring some 215 times in the Qurʾan and functioning as: I declinable interrogative pronoun always in a construct (اسم استفهام معرب) ‘who?’, ‘which?’, ‘what?’ and used for both rational beings (9:124) وَإِذَا مَآ أُنزِلَتْ سُورَةٌ فَمِنْهُم مَّن يَقُولُ أَيُّكُمْ زَادَتْهُ هَٰذِهِۦٓ إِيمَٰنًا when a sura is revealed, some of them say, ‘Which of you has this increased in faith’; and for non-rational beings (77:50) فَبِأَىِّ حَدِيثٍۭ بَعْدَهُۥ يُؤْمِنُونَ so in what discourse, after it, will they believe? In connected discourse this أَيّ may lose its interrogative function but remains as a determiner. This may occur when أَيّ itself becomes governed by, e.g., a verb (26:227) وَسَيَعْلَمُ ٱلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوٓا۟ أَىَّ مُنقَلَبٍ يَنقَلِبُونَ the evildoers will come to know what place of returning they will return to; or when the entire أَيّ phrase is governed by, (or subordinated to) a preceding verb (4:11) ءَابَآؤُكُمْ وَأَبْنَآؤُكُمْ لَا تَدْرُونَ أَيُّهُمْ أَقْرَبُ لَكُمْ نَفْعًا your parents and your children–you do not know which is more likely [lit. nearer] to you in usefulness. This أَيّ occurs inflected for feminine gender, أَيَّة ʾayyati, only in the variant non-popular reading of ʾIbn Masʿūd of (31:34) وَمَا تَدْرِي نَفْسٌ بِأَيَّةِ أَرْضٍ تَمُوتُ and no soul knows in what land it will die II a declinable conditional (شرطية معربة) always in construct with a following nominal ‘whichever’, ‘whoever’, occurring in the Qurʾan with a following mā of ambiguity (ما المبهمة) which lends emphasis to the entire sentence (28:28) أَيَّمَا الأَجَلَيْنِ قَضَيْتُ فَلَا عُدْوَٰنَ عَلَىَّ whichever of the two terms I fulfil, let there be no sanction against me III a declinable relative pronoun (اسم موصول معرب) ‘who’, ‘which’, ‘whom’ (17:57) أُولَٰئِكَ ٱلَّذِينَ يَدْعُونَ يَبْتَغُونَ إِلَىٰ رَبِّهِمُ ٱلْوَسِيلَةَ


أَيُّهُمْ أَقْرَبُ those upon whom they [the disbelievers] call—
[themselves] seek a way to their Lord, even those of them who are
closest [to Him] IV a prefixal connector to a noun bearing the
article ʾal as a means of calling it (وصلة إلى نداء ما فيه ال) as part of
the vocative (انظر ... أيا ...) (including relative pronouns with ʾal such
as الذي ʾalladhī). This أَيّ is always suffixed with attention-drawing
hā (q.v.) and is inflected for gender (أَيُّهَا ʾayyuhā and أَيَّتُهَا
ʾayyatuhā) (2:21) يَا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ اعْبُدُوا رَبَّكُمْ people, worship your Lord;
(12:70) يَا أَيَّتُهَا الْعِيرُ إِنَّكُمْ لَسَارِقُونَ you camel riders!, you are thieves. It is
often used interjectionally (15:6) وَقَالُوا يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِي نُزِّلَ عَلَيْهِ الذِّكْرُ إِنَّكَ
لَمَجْنُونٌ they say, ‘You, to whom the Reminder has been revealed!,
you are certainly mad’. In three places in the Qurʾanic text أَيُّهَا
ʾayyuhā occurs orthographically as أَيُّهَ ayyuha with a final short
vowel /a/, reflecting the actual shortening of the final long vowel
in connected speech (55:31) سَنَفْرُغُ لَكُمْ أَيُّهَ الثَّقَلَانِ We will attend to
you both [We shall take you to task], you two groups [of jinn and
mankind] laden [with responsibilities] V [prefixed with ك with
the compensation nunation (تنوين التعويض) (q.v.) noted as ان]
exclamatory ‘how much!’, ‘how many!’ (للتكثير) (22:48) وَكَأَيِّن مِّنْ
قَرْيَةٍ أَمْلَيْتُ لَهَا وَهِيَ ظَالِمَةٌ how many a town steeped in wrongdoing I
gave more time to.

أَيَّةِ ʾayyati (a variant reading of أَيّ ʾayy; see: أَيّ ʾayy).

أَيَّتُهَا ʾayyatuhā (see أَيّ ʾayy).

أَيُّمَا ʾayyumā (see أَيّ ʾayy).

أَيُّهَ ʾayyuha (see أَيّ ʾayy).

أَيُّهَا ʾayyuhā (see أَيّ ʾayy).

أَيُّوبُ ʾayyūb [a borrowed proper name occurring four times in the
Qurʾan] Prophet Job (Job III) (38:41) وَاذْكُرْ عَبْدَنَا أَيُّوبَ إِذْ نَادَىٰ رَبَّهُ أَنِّي
مَسَّنِيَ الشَّيْطَانُ بِنُصْبٍ وَعَذَابٍ and remember Our servant Job when he
called to his Lord, ‘Satan has afflicted me with weariness and
suffering.’

71

أيان

Job is named in the distinguished company of prophets whom
God had inspired (4:163), and in one extended narrative, (38:41–
4) his suffering and patience serve as a powerful symbolic
parallel with the Prophet Muḥammad’s own struggle. In answer
to his prayers, God provides him with ‘cool water’ with which he
washes himself and drinks. He is cured as a token of God’s mercy
and has his family restored to him.

إِيَّا ʾiyyā detached pronoun in the accusative case specified for gender,
number and person in accordance with the suffix to which it is
always attached (ضمير نصب منفصل) (16:51) فَإِيَّايَ فَارْهَبُونِ I alone am
the One that you should hold in awe. The use of this detached
pronoun, as contrasted with its attached counterpart lends great
emphasis to the context (e.g. إِيَّاكَ ʾiyyāka ‘you alone!’), as in (1:5)
إِيَّاكَ نَعْبُدُ وَإِيَّاكَ نَسْتَعِينُ it is You we worship; it is You we ask for help.

أَيَّانَ ʾayyāna (see أ/و/ي–ن ʾ–w/y–n).

72

ب / bāʾ

اَلْبَاءُ ʾal-bāʾ the second letter of the alphabet; it represents a voiced
bi-labial plosive sound.

بِ bi preposition (حرف جرّ) occurring in 2538 places in the Qurʾan. In
context it conveys various meanings among which are the
following: 1 accompaniment, or concomitance (المصاحبة), ‘with’
(11:48) يَا نُوحُ اهْبِطْ بِسَلَامٍ مِّنَّا وَبَرَكَاتٍ Noah, descend with peace from Us
and blessings 2 instrument or means by which the action is
performed (الاستعانة), ‘by means of’, ‘by the help of’ (1:1) بِسْمِ اللَّهِ
الرَّحْمَنِ الرَّحِيمِ [I begin/act] with the help of the name of God, the
Lord of mercy, the Giver of mercy 3 indicating the reason for the
action (السببية), ‘because of’, ‘for’, ‘as consequence of’ (29:40) فَكُلًّا
أَخَذْنَا بِذَنبِهِ and We punished each one of them for their sins 4
indicating … ‘in return for’, ‘in exchange for’ (المقابلة) (16:32)
ادْخُلُوا الْجَنَّةَ بِمَا كُنتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ enter the Garden in return for what you
have done 5 indicating location (الظرفية) on a) a surface ‘on’
(2:265) كَمَثَلِ جَنَّةٍ بِرَبْوَةٍ like a garden on a hill b) in an area ‘at’
(3:123) وَلَقَدْ نَصَرَكُمُ اللَّهُ بِبَدْرٍ and God indeed helped you at Badr; or
c) at a point in time ‘at the time of’ (54:34) إِلَّا آلَ لُوطٍ نَّجَّيْنَاهُم بِسَحَرٍ
except the family of Lot whom We rescued at the last hour of the
night 6 indicating the target or recipient of the action (الغاية),
‘towards’, ‘to’ (12:100) وَقَدْ أَحْسَنَ بِي إِذْ أَخْرَجَنِي مِنَ السِّجْنِ and had been
gracious to me—when He released me from prison 7 indicating
elevation (الاستعلاء) a) physical ‘above’, ‘on top of’ (4:42) لَوْ تُسَوَّىٰ
بِهِمُ الْأَرْضُ that the earth is levelled over them or b) abstract, in the
meaning of prep. على ‘over’; ‘on’ (3:75) وَمِنْهُم مَّنْ إِن تَأْمَنْهُ بِدِينَارٍ لَّا
يُؤَدِّهِ إِلَيْكَ but of them is he who, if you trust him with [lit. over] a
[single] dinar, will not return it to you or c) indicating the mere

73

بابل

surface, also one of the senses of prep. على, ‘over’ (4:43) فَامْسَحُوا
بِوُجُوهِكُمْ وَأَيْدِيكُمْ and wipe over your faces and hands [with it] 8 the
sense of prep. min (بمعنى من), ‘from’ (76:6) عَيْنًا يَشْرَبُ بِهَا عِبَادُ اللَّهِ a
spring from which the servants of God drink 9 adhesiveness,
indicating firm or close contact (الإلصاق), ‘by’ (7:150) وَأَخَذَ بِرَأْسِ
أَخِيهِ يَجُرُّهُ إِلَيْهِ and he took hold of his brother, dragging him to himself
by the head 10 the sense of prep. ʿan (بمعنى عن), ‘about’,
‘concerning’ (25:59) الرَّحْمَنِ فَاسْأَلْ بِهِ خَبِيرًا the Lord of Mercy; ask
any informed person about Him 11 ‘passing by’ (83:30) وَإِذَا مَرُّوا
بِهِمْ يَتَغَامَزُونَ and when they pass by them they wink at one another 12
‘notwithstanding’, ‘for all’, ‘in spite of’ (9:118) ضَاقَتْ عَلَيْهِمُ الْأَرْضُ
بِمَا رَحُبَتْ when the earth, for all its spaciousness, closed in around
them 13 over and above, in addition to (in one interpretation of
[[unclear: 3:153]]) فَأَثَابَكُمْ غَمًّا بِغَمٍّ so He repaid you with grief over
grief 14 oath (القسم) (38:82) قَالَ فَبِعِزَّتِكَ لَأُغْوِيَنَّهُمْ أَجْمَعِينَ he
said, ‘By your might I will tempt them all’ 15 transitivity
(التعدية), rendering a verb transitive (77:50) فَبِأَيِّ حَدِيثٍ بَعْدَهُ
يُؤْمِنُونَ so in what discourse, after it, will they believe?; also in (2:17)
ذَهَبَ اللَّهُ بِنُورِهِمْ God took away their light 16 emphasis (التأكيد)
(the so-called ‘redundant’ bāʾ) (الباء الزائدة) (13:43) قُلْ كَفَىٰ بِاللَّهِ
شَهِيدًا بَيْنِي وَبَيْنَكُمْ say, ‘[Indeed], God suffices as a witness
between me and you’. Emphatic ب occurs often in the compliment
of negative particles as in (81:25) وَمَا هُوَ بِقَوْلِ شَيْطَانٍ رَّجِيمٍ this is in
no way the speech of any accursed devil 17 the baʾ of
wonder, of interjection (باء التعجب) (18:26) أَبْصِرْ بِهِ وَأَسْمِعْ how well
He sees and hears!

بَابِلُ Bābil [proper name, early borrowing from Akkadian, occurring
once in the Qurʾan. Philologists derive it from ب/ب/ل b–b–l
although they recognize the word as the name of a city/region in
Iraq famous for wine and magic] Babylon, the word occurs in
reference to two angels, Hārūt and Mārūt, who were sent to try
the faith of the inhabitants of the city of Babylon. They taught the
inhabitants magic while at the same time warning them that, ‘We
are only a test, so do not abandon your beliefs’, but they learnt
from them harmful acts of magic such as how to sow discord
between a man and his wife (2:102) وَمَا أُنزِلَ عَلَى الْمَلَكَيْنِ بِبَابِلَ هَارُوتَ
وَمَارُوتَ and what was revealed to the two angels, Hārūt and

74
entries.definition_english:
ʾ–y–y (1) marker, sign, token, indicator, to mark; to pause, to tarry; to ponder, to assure oneself; a person’s body; glory; light of the sun; beauty of flowers. Of this root, three forms occur 380 times in the Qurʾan: آيَة ʾāyatun 86 times; آيَتَيْن ʾāyatayn once and آيَات ʾāyāt 295 times.
آيَة ʾāyatun [n.; dual آيَتَيْن ʾāyatayn; pl. آيَات ʾāyāt] 1 sign, indicator, indication (12:105) وَكَأَيِّن مِّنْ ءَايَةٍ فِى ٱلسَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ يَمُرُّونَ عَلَيْهَا وَهُمْ عَنْهَا مُعْرِضُونَ and how many a sign is there in the heavens and the Earth which they pass by, turning away from it! 2 evidence, proof (12:35) ثُمَّ بَدَا لَهُم مِّنۢ بَعْدِ مَا رَأَوُا۟ ٱلْءَايَٰتِ لَيَسْجُنُنَّهُۥ حَتَّىٰ حِينٍ but then it occurred to them, [even] after seeing the evidence [for his innocence], that they should imprison him for a while 3 miracle, portent (13:7) وَيَقُولُ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ لَوْلَآ أُنزِلَ عَلَيْهِ ءَايَةٌ مِّن رَّبِّهِۦ and those who disbelieve say, ‘If only some portent were sent down upon him from his Lord?’ 4 symbol, exemplar (23:50) وَجَعَلْنَا ٱبْنَ مَرْيَمَ وَأُمَّهُۥٓ ءَايَةً and We made the son of Mary and his mother an exemplar 5 message, revelation (20:134) رَبَّنَا لَوْلَآ أَرْسَلْتَ إِلَيْنَا رَسُولًا فَنَتَّبِعَ ءَايَٰتِكَ مِن قَبْلِ أَن نَّذِلَّ وَنَخْزَىٰ Lord, if only You had sent us a messenger, so that we might have followed Your revelations, before we suffered humiliation and disgrace! 6 teachings, instructions (24:1) سُورَةٌ أَنزَلْنَٰهَا وَفَرَضْنَٰهَا وَأَنزَلْنَا فِيهَآ ءَايَٰتٍ بَيِّنَٰتٍ this is a sura We have sent down and made obligatory–We have sent down clear instruction in it, so that you may take heed 7 verses, parts of the Qurʾan (16:101) وَإِذَا بَدَّلْنَآ ءَايَةً مَّكَانَ ءَايَةٍ وَٱللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ بِمَا يُنَزِّلُ قَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّمَآ أَنتَ مُفْتَرٍ when We substitute one verse for another–and God knows best what He reveals–they say, ‘You are just making it up’ 8

69

أَيّ

best what He reveals–they say, ‘You are just making it up’ 8 lesson (3:13) قَدْ كَانَ لَكُمْ ءَايَةٌ فِى فِئَتَيْنِ ٱلْتَقَتَا you have already seen a lesson in the two armies that met [in battle] 9 glory, wonder (17:1) سُبْحَٰنَ ٱلَّذِىٓ أَسْرَىٰ بِعَبْدِهِۦ لَيْلًا مِّنَ ٱلْمَسْجِدِ ٱلْحَرَامِ إِلَى ٱلْمَسْجِدِ ٱلْأَقْصَا ٱلَّذِى بَٰرَكْنَا حَوْلَهُۥ لِنُرِيَهُۥ مِنْ ءَايَٰتِنَآ glory be to Him who made His servant travel by night from the Sacred Mosque to the Furthest Mosque, whose surroundings We have blessed, to show him some of Our wonders 10 spell, in an interpretation of verse (7:132) وَقَالُوا۟ مَهْمَا تَأْتِنَا بِهِۦ مِنْ ءَايَةٍ لِّتَسْحَرَنَا بِهَا فَمَا نَحْنُ لَكَ بِمُؤْمِنِينَ and they said ‘Whatever spell [also interpreted as: sign] you produce in order to enchant us with, we will never believe with you.’

أَيّ ʾayy (2) occurring some 215 times in the Qurʾan and functioning as: I declinable interrogative pronoun always in a construct (اسم استفهام معرب) ‘who?’, ‘which?’, ‘what?’ and used for both rational beings (9:124) وَإِذَا مَآ أُنزِلَتْ سُورَةٌ فَمِنْهُم مَّن يَقُولُ أَيُّكُمْ زَادَتْهُ هَٰذِهِۦٓ إِيمَٰنًا when a sura is revealed, some of them say, ‘Which of you has this increased in faith’; and for non-rational beings (77:50) فَبِأَىِّ حَدِيثٍۭ بَعْدَهُۥ يُؤْمِنُونَ so in what discourse, after it, will they believe? In connected discourse this أَيّ may lose its interrogative function but remains as a determiner. This may occur when أَيّ itself becomes governed by, e.g., a verb (26:227) وَسَيَعْلَمُ ٱلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوٓا۟ أَىَّ مُنقَلَبٍ يَنقَلِبُونَ the evildoers will come to know what place of returning they will return to; or when the entire أَيّ phrase is governed by, (or subordinated to) a preceding verb (4:11) ءَابَآؤُكُمْ وَأَبْنَآؤُكُمْ لَا تَدْرُونَ أَيُّهُمْ أَقْرَبُ لَكُمْ نَفْعًا your parents and your children–you do not know which is more likely [lit. nearer] to you in usefulness. This أَيّ occurs inflected for feminine gender, أَيَّة ʾayyati, only in the variant non-popular reading of ʾIbn Masʿūd of (31:34) وَمَا تَدْرِي نَفْسٌ بِأَيَّةِ أَرْضٍ تَمُوتُ and no soul knows in what land it will die II a declinable conditional (شرطية معربة) always in construct with a following nominal ‘whichever’, ‘whoever’, occurring in the Qurʾan with a following mā of ambiguity (ما المبهمة) which lends emphasis to the entire sentence (28:28) أَيَّمَا الأَجَلَيْنِ قَضَيْتُ فَلَا عُدْوَٰنَ عَلَىَّ whichever of the two terms I fulfil, let there be no sanction against me III a declinable relative pronoun (اسم موصول معرب) ‘who’, ‘which’, ‘whom’ (17:57) أُولَٰئِكَ ٱلَّذِينَ يَدْعُونَ يَبْتَغُونَ إِلَىٰ رَبِّهِمُ ٱلْوَسِيلَةَ


أَيُّهُمْ أَقْرَبُ those upon whom they [the disbelievers] call—
[themselves] seek a way to their Lord, even those of them who are
closest [to Him] IV a prefixal connector to a noun bearing the
article ʾal as a means of calling it (وصلة إلى نداء ما فيه ال) as part of
the vocative (انظر ... أيا ...) (including relative pronouns with ʾal such
as الذي ʾalladhī). This أَيّ is always suffixed with attention-drawing
hā (q.v.) and is inflected for gender (أَيُّهَا ʾayyuhā and أَيَّتُهَا
ʾayyatuhā) (2:21) يَا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ اعْبُدُوا رَبَّكُمْ people, worship your Lord;
(12:70) يَا أَيَّتُهَا الْعِيرُ إِنَّكُمْ لَسَارِقُونَ you camel riders!, you are thieves. It is
often used interjectionally (15:6) وَقَالُوا يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِي نُزِّلَ عَلَيْهِ الذِّكْرُ إِنَّكَ
لَمَجْنُونٌ they say, ‘You, to whom the Reminder has been revealed!,
you are certainly mad’. In three places in the Qurʾanic text أَيُّهَا
ʾayyuhā occurs orthographically as أَيُّهَ ayyuha with a final short
vowel /a/, reflecting the actual shortening of the final long vowel
in connected speech (55:31) سَنَفْرُغُ لَكُمْ أَيُّهَ الثَّقَلَانِ We will attend to
you both [We shall take you to task], you two groups [of jinn and
mankind] laden [with responsibilities] V [prefixed with ك with
the compensation nunation (تنوين التعويض) (q.v.) noted as ان]
exclamatory ‘how much!’, ‘how many!’ (للتكثير) (22:48) وَكَأَيِّن مِّنْ
قَرْيَةٍ أَمْلَيْتُ لَهَا وَهِيَ ظَالِمَةٌ how many a town steeped in wrongdoing I
gave more time to.

أَيَّةِ ʾayyati (a variant reading of أَيّ ʾayy; see: أَيّ ʾayy).

أَيَّتُهَا ʾayyatuhā (see أَيّ ʾayy).

أَيُّمَا ʾayyumā (see أَيّ ʾayy).

أَيُّهَ ʾayyuha (see أَيّ ʾayy).

أَيُّهَا ʾayyuhā (see أَيّ ʾayy).

أَيُّوبُ ʾayyūb [a borrowed proper name occurring four times in the
Qurʾan] Prophet Job (Job III) (38:41) وَاذْكُرْ عَبْدَنَا أَيُّوبَ إِذْ نَادَىٰ رَبَّهُ أَنِّي
مَسَّنِيَ الشَّيْطَانُ بِنُصْبٍ وَعَذَابٍ and remember Our servant Job when he
called to his Lord, ‘Satan has afflicted me with weariness and
suffering.’

71

أيان

Job is named in the distinguished company of prophets whom
God had inspired (4:163), and in one extended narrative, (38:41–
4) his suffering and patience serve as a powerful symbolic
parallel with the Prophet Muḥammad’s own struggle. In answer
to his prayers, God provides him with ‘cool water’ with which he
washes himself and drinks. He is cured as a token of God’s mercy
and has his family restored to him.

إِيَّا ʾiyyā detached pronoun in the accusative case specified for gender,
number and person in accordance with the suffix to which it is
always attached (ضمير نصب منفصل) (16:51) فَإِيَّايَ فَارْهَبُونِ I alone am
the One that you should hold in awe. The use of this detached
pronoun, as contrasted with its attached counterpart lends great
emphasis to the context (e.g. إِيَّاكَ ʾiyyāka ‘you alone!’), as in (1:5)
إِيَّاكَ نَعْبُدُ وَإِيَّاكَ نَسْتَعِينُ it is You we worship; it is You we ask for help.

أَيَّانَ ʾayyāna (see أ/و/ي–ن ʾ–w/y–n).

72

ب / bāʾ

اَلْبَاءُ ʾal-bāʾ the second letter of the alphabet; it represents a voiced
bi-labial plosive sound.

بِ bi preposition (حرف جرّ) occurring in 2538 places in the Qurʾan. In
context it conveys various meanings among which are the
following: 1 accompaniment, or concomitance (المصاحبة), ‘with’
(11:48) يَا نُوحُ اهْبِطْ بِسَلَامٍ مِّنَّا وَبَرَكَاتٍ Noah, descend with peace from Us
and blessings 2 instrument or means by which the action is
performed (الاستعانة), ‘by means of’, ‘by the help of’ (1:1) بِسْمِ اللَّهِ
الرَّحْمَنِ الرَّحِيمِ [I begin/act] with the help of the name of God, the
Lord of mercy, the Giver of mercy 3 indicating the reason for the
action (السببية), ‘because of’, ‘for’, ‘as consequence of’ (29:40) فَكُلًّا
أَخَذْنَا بِذَنبِهِ and We punished each one of them for their sins 4
indicating … ‘in return for’, ‘in exchange for’ (المقابلة) (16:32)
ادْخُلُوا الْجَنَّةَ بِمَا كُنتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ enter the Garden in return for what you
have done 5 indicating location (الظرفية) on a) a surface ‘on’
(2:265) كَمَثَلِ جَنَّةٍ بِرَبْوَةٍ like a garden on a hill b) in an area ‘at’
(3:123) وَلَقَدْ نَصَرَكُمُ اللَّهُ بِبَدْرٍ and God indeed helped you at Badr; or
c) at a point in time ‘at the time of’ (54:34) إِلَّا آلَ لُوطٍ نَّجَّيْنَاهُم بِسَحَرٍ
except the family of Lot whom We rescued at the last hour of the
night 6 indicating the target or recipient of the action (الغاية),
‘towards’, ‘to’ (12:100) وَقَدْ أَحْسَنَ بِي إِذْ أَخْرَجَنِي مِنَ السِّجْنِ and had been
gracious to me—when He released me from prison 7 indicating
elevation (الاستعلاء) a) physical ‘above’, ‘on top of’ (4:42) لَوْ تُسَوَّىٰ
بِهِمُ الْأَرْضُ that the earth is levelled over them or b) abstract, in the
meaning of prep. على ‘over’; ‘on’ (3:75) وَمِنْهُم مَّنْ إِن تَأْمَنْهُ بِدِينَارٍ لَّا
يُؤَدِّهِ إِلَيْكَ but of them is he who, if you trust him with [lit. over] a
[single] dinar, will not return it to you or c) indicating the mere

73

بابل

surface, also one of the senses of prep. على, ‘over’ (4:43) فَامْسَحُوا
بِوُجُوهِكُمْ وَأَيْدِيكُمْ and wipe over your faces and hands [with it] 8 the
sense of prep. min (بمعنى من), ‘from’ (76:6) عَيْنًا يَشْرَبُ بِهَا عِبَادُ اللَّهِ a
spring from which the servants of God drink 9 adhesiveness,
indicating firm or close contact (الإلصاق), ‘by’ (7:150) وَأَخَذَ بِرَأْسِ
أَخِيهِ يَجُرُّهُ إِلَيْهِ and he took hold of his brother, dragging him to himself
by the head 10 the sense of prep. ʿan (بمعنى عن), ‘about’,
‘concerning’ (25:59) الرَّحْمَنِ فَاسْأَلْ بِهِ خَبِيرًا the Lord of Mercy; ask
any informed person about Him 11 ‘passing by’ (83:30) وَإِذَا مَرُّوا
بِهِمْ يَتَغَامَزُونَ and when they pass by them they wink at one another 12
‘notwithstanding’, ‘for all’, ‘in spite of’ (9:118) ضَاقَتْ عَلَيْهِمُ الْأَرْضُ
بِمَا رَحُبَتْ when the earth, for all its spaciousness, closed in around
them 13 over and above, in addition to (in one interpretation of
[[unclear: 3:153]]) فَأَثَابَكُمْ غَمًّا بِغَمٍّ so He repaid you with grief over
grief 14 oath (القسم) (38:82) قَالَ فَبِعِزَّتِكَ لَأُغْوِيَنَّهُمْ أَجْمَعِينَ he
said, ‘By your might I will tempt them all’ 15 transitivity
(التعدية), rendering a verb transitive (77:50) فَبِأَيِّ حَدِيثٍ بَعْدَهُ
يُؤْمِنُونَ so in what discourse, after it, will they believe?; also in (2:17)
ذَهَبَ اللَّهُ بِنُورِهِمْ God took away their light 16 emphasis (التأكيد)
(the so-called ‘redundant’ bāʾ) (الباء الزائدة) (13:43) قُلْ كَفَىٰ بِاللَّهِ
شَهِيدًا بَيْنِي وَبَيْنَكُمْ say, ‘[Indeed], God suffices as a witness
between me and you’. Emphatic ب occurs often in the compliment
of negative particles as in (81:25) وَمَا هُوَ بِقَوْلِ شَيْطَانٍ رَّجِيمٍ this is in
no way the speech of any accursed devil 17 the baʾ of
wonder, of interjection (باء التعجب) (18:26) أَبْصِرْ بِهِ وَأَسْمِعْ how well
He sees and hears!

بَابِلُ Bābil [proper name, early borrowing from Akkadian, occurring
once in the Qurʾan. Philologists derive it from ب/ب/ل b–b–l
although they recognize the word as the name of a city/region in
Iraq famous for wine and magic] Babylon, the word occurs in
reference to two angels, Hārūt and Mārūt, who were sent to try
the faith of the inhabitants of the city of Babylon. They taught the
inhabitants magic while at the same time warning them that, ‘We
are only a test, so do not abandon your beliefs’, but they learnt
from them harmful acts of magic such as how to sow discord
between a man and his wife (2:102) وَمَا أُنزِلَ عَلَى الْمَلَكَيْنِ بِبَابِلَ هَارُوتَ
وَمَارُوتَ and what was revealed to the two angels, Hārūt and

74
